sentences.text: Cancer Vaccines Number: 0557 Applicable CPT / HCPCS / ICD-10 Codes Scope of Policy This Clinical Policy Bulletin addresses cancer vaccines.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: I. Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Aetna considers the following cancer vaccines experimental, investigational, or unproven because the safety and effectiveness of these approaches has not been established: A. Helper multi-peptide (6MHP) vaccine for metastatic melanoma B. HLA-A24-binding peptide vaccines for gastric cancer C. Melanoma vaccine (also known as Theraccine vaccine or Oncophage vaccine) for any indication D. p16(INK4a)-based peptide vaccine for human papillomavirus-associated cancers (e.g., anal, cervical, oropharyngeal, penile, rectal, vaginal, and vulvar cancers) E. p62 DNA vaccine (Elenagen) for solid tumors (e.g., breast, kidney, lung, and ovary cancers as well as melanoma) F. Use of whole cancer stem cell-based vaccines as an adjuvant strategy for the surgical management of solid tumors G. Vaccine therapy including tumor-associated antigenic peptide-based vaccines in the treatment of the following cancers (not an all-inclusive list) because the clinical evidence is not sufficient to permit conclusions on the health outcome effects of vaccine therapy in the treatment of these cancers: Acute myeloid leukemia Breast cancer Central nervous system cancers (e.g., glioblastoma and neuroblastoma) Colorectal cancer Gallbladder cancer Gastric cancer Glioma Head and neck cancer Hepatic cancer Lung cancer Oral squamous cell carcinoma Ovarian cancer Pancreatic cancer.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Table: CPT Codes / HCPCS Codes / ICD-10 Codes Code Code Description There is no specific CPT code for melanoma vaccine (e.g., Theraccine vaccine or Oncophage vaccine, cancer vaccine therapy, helper multi-peptide (6MHP) vaccine, or vaccine therapy including tumor-associated antigenic peptide-based vaccines): CPT codes not covered for indications listed in the CPB: HLA-A-24-binding peptide vaccines, p16(INK4a)-based peptide vaccine, p62 DNA vaccine (Elenagen), Stem cell-based vaccines - no specific code: ICD-10 codes not covered for indications listed in the CPB: Melanoma of ovary or kidney - no specific code : C00.0 – C96.9 Malignant neoplasms D00.00 - D09.9 Carcinoma in situ Z23 Encounter for immunization Melanoma Vaccine Melanoma vaccine is prepared from melanoma cell lines that are cultured in-vitro.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The vaccine product is designed to contain 1 or more antigens that are unique to melanoma cells; some preparations also contain "adjuvants" (such as bacillus Calmette-Guerin [BCG]) thought to enhance immunogenicity of the preparation.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Currently, no melanoma vaccine products are licensed for marketing in the United States.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Until a commercial preparation is available, there is significant potential for variability between products and batches.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Effects that are seen in patients to whom these products are administered may be due to the components thought to be active, or to other components which are contaminants, adjuvants or excipients.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In those studies lacking a concurrent control group, it is impossible to distinguish which components are active.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ribi Immunochem Research has developed a standardized melanoma vaccine called Melacine.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: As a biological, this product would be evaluated and licensed by the Center for Biological Evaluation and Research of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: At this time, no application for licensure has been filed for this product.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Several published studies have suggested that melanoma vaccine may be effective in the treatment of malignant melanoma and/or prevention of metastasis of melanoma; however, these studies are not adequate to establish the safety and/or efficacy of this therapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, there have been a number of reports of phase I and phase II studies that have demonstrated increases in tumor-specific cytotoxic T lymphocytes and other immune responses in patients vaccinated with various melanoma vaccines.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A number of these studies have incorporated interleukin-2 and other adjuvants to improve immune response to melanoma vaccine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The second interim report from a phase III, multi-center randomized controlled clinical trial of a melanoma vaccine, however, showed no significant overall survival benefit in vaccinated melanoma patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A retrospective analysis of clinical trial results identified subsets of patients that appeared to have improved survival with the vaccine; this retrospective analysis suggests that a vaccine may be effective in selected subgroups of melanoma patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Randomized controlled clinical trials, where subgroups of patients are identified prospectively, are necessary to confirm the efficacy of melanoma vaccine in selected patient subgroups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Kuhn and Hanke (1997) concluded in a recent review of the current status of melanoma vaccines that "[t]he clinical effectiveness of melanoma vaccines is unclear and adequately controlled studies need yet to be performed".
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A former President of the American Cancer Society stated that "[t]he success of melanoma vaccines has been inhibited by the large number of potential antigen targets on the melanoma cell, which may vary from tumor to tumor.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The FDA orphan drug designation was designed to encourage the development of therapies for conditions affecting fewer than 200,000 people in the United States.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Oncophage vaccine is prepared by removing a tumor from the patient and isolate specific antigens of the particular cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: According to Antigenics, Inc., studies have shown that the protein complex, when purified from tumor cells and reintroduced to the patient, appears to stimulate cellular immunity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The immune response appears to be directed specifically to cancer cells, leaving the patient's healthy tissue intact.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In May 2002, Antigenics reported that it had begun enrolling patients in the United States and Europe in a pivotal phase III trial of Oncophage for stage IV metastatic melanoma.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Elliott and Dalgleish (2004) stated that melanoma vaccines offer new hope to patients with metastatic melanoma, although convincing survival advantages have yet to be reported.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bystryn and Reynolds (2005) stated that vaccines are a promising but still experimental treatment for melanoma.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Guidelines on malignant melanoma from the National Institute for Health and Clinical Excellence (2006) have concluded that "[v]accine therapy for advanced [malignant melanoma] remains uncertain and its use should only be in the context of a clinical trial."
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Melanoma vaccines can be classified into 6 groups: I. whole-cell vaccines, II. dendritic cell vaccines, III. peptide vaccines, IV.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: ganglioside vaccines, V. DNA vaccines and VI. viral vectors. I. whole-cell vaccines, II. dendritic cell vaccines, III. peptide vaccines, IV.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: ganglioside vaccines, V. DNA vaccines and VI. viral vectors.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The main characteristics of these vaccines including their advantages and disadvantages and the results from conducted trials were presented.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Clinical responses to melanoma vaccines are still poor and currently there is no melanoma vaccine with a proven efficacy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This is in agreement with the observations of Stein and Brownell (2008) who noted that advanced melanoma has a poor prognosis, and standard adjuvant treatment offers little survival advantage.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Lesterhuis and associates (2008) stated that dendritic cells are the directors of the immune system, capable of inducing tumor antigen-specific T-cell and B-cell responses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In recent years, additional trials have been carried out in melanoma patients, and although immunological responses are often reported, objective clinical responses remain anecdotal with objective response rates not exceeding 5 to 10 %.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Thus, dendritic cells vaccination research has now entered a stage in between "proof of principle" and "proof of efficacy" trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Faries et al (2009) examined the effect of the addition of granulocyte/macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) to vaccination with a melanoma vaccine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The GM-CSF arm showed enhanced antibody responses with an increase in IgM titer against the TA90 antigen and increased TA90 immune complexes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Peripheral blood leukocyte profiles showed increases in eosinophils and basophils with decreased monocytes in the GM-CSF arm.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 121 eligible patients with resected stage IIB to IV melanoma were vaccinated with 12 major histocompatibility complex class I-restricted melanoma peptides to stimulate CD8+ T cells plus a human leukocyte antigen (HLA)-DR-restricted tetanus helper peptide to stimulate CD4+ T cells, emulsified in incomplete Freund's adjuvant, with or without 110 microg GM-CSF.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Among 119 evaluable patients, T-cell responses were assessed by IFN-gamma ELIspot assay and tetramer analysis. Clinical outcomes were recorded.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CD8+ T-cell response rates to the 12 MHC class I-restricted melanoma peptides (by day 50) with or without GM-CSF were 34 % and 73 %, respectively (p < 0.001), by direct ELIspot assay.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: CD4+ T-cell responses to tetanus helper peptide were higher without GM-CSF (95 % versus 77 %; p = 0.005).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Three-year overall and disease-free survival estimates (95 % confidence interval) were 76 % (67 % to 83 %) and 52 % (43 % to 61 %), respectively, with too few events to assess differences by study group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that high immune response rates for this multi- peptide vaccine were achieved, but CD8+ and CD4+ T-cell responses were lower when administered with GM-CSF.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Kaufman (2012) noted that the inherent immunogenicity of melanoma and renal cell carcinoma (RCC) has made these tumors a focus of considerable research in vaccine development.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Melanoma has been a useful model for the identification of tumor-associated antigens and a number of putative renal cell antigens have been described more recently.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These antigens have been targeted using a variety of vaccine strategies, including protein- and peptide-based vaccines, recombinant antigen-expressing vectors, and whole cell vaccine approaches.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In general, ovarian tumors are named according to the kind of cells the tumor started from and whether the tumor is benign or cancerous.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There are 3 main types of ovarian tumors: I. germ cell tumors start from the cells that produce the ova (eggs); II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: stromal tumors start from connective tissue cells that hold the ovary together and produce the female hormones estrogen and progesterone; III.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: epithelial tumors start from the cells that cover the outer surface of the ovary.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Therapeutic vaccines are intended to coerce the cellular components of the immune system to attack malignant tissue.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Prophylactic vaccines are intended to induce the production of antibodies capable of neutralizing viral antigens before they infect host cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The National Cancer Institute Ovarian Epithelial Cancer Treatment PDQ (2008) states that vaccines are under clinical evaluation in the treatment of ovarian cancer, primarily as part of consolidation therapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Antigen-specific active immunotherapy aims to induce a tumor-antigen-specific anti-tumor immune responses as an alternative treatment for ovarian cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a review, Gardner and Jewell (2011) wrote that future trials will determine the role of biologic agents and vaccine therapies for ovarian cancer, as well as their impact on quality of life.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vaccines for Selected Types of Cancer Krishnadas et al (2013) stated that patients with relapsed stage 4 neuroblastoma have an extremely poor long- term prognosis, making the investigation of new agents of interest.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These investigators reported the outcome of the first patient treated in a phase I study for relapsed neuroblastoma, using the chemotherapy agent decitabine to up-regulate cancer testis antigen expression, followed by a dendritic cell vaccine targeting the cancer testis antigens MAGE-A1, MAGE-A3, and NY-ESO-1.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The patient in this study had persistent tumor in his bone marrow after completion of standard therapy for neuroblastoma, including multi-agent chemotherapy, tumor resection, stem cell transplantation, radiation therapy, and anti-GD2 monoclonal antibodies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: After 3 cycles of decitabine and vaccine, this patient achieved a complete remission and is now 1 year from his last treatment, with no evidence of tumor in his bone marrow or other sites.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This patient was noted to have an increase in MAGE-A3-specific T cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This was the first report combining demethylating chemotherapy to enhance tumor antigen expression followed by a cancer antigen vaccine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Hall et al (2013) discussed recent clinical trials using immunotherapy techniques to treat both non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and small cell lung cancer (SCLC) and high-lighted ongoing immunotherapy research efforts at the authors’ center.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: For NSCLC, phase II clinical trials have examined allogeneic vaccines that target either mucin 1 (MUC1), epidermal growth factor or melanoma-associated antigen 3.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: An autologous cellular therapy directed against transforming growth factor beta-2 and a recombinant protein with antitumor properties have also shown promise in prolonging survival in NSCLC in phase II trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The monoclonal antibodies ipilimumab, BMS-936558 (anti-PD-1), and BMS936559 (anti-PD-L1) lead to enhanced T-cell-mediated anti-tumor effects and have produced objective responses in early-phase clinical trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that recent clinical trials in lung cancer demonstrated the potential of immunotherapeutics to increase overall survival in patients with lung cancer compared with the current standard of care.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Advances in DNA and RNA sequencing and drug development may also ultimately allow for the creation of personalized vaccines consisting of several antigens uniquely expressed by an individual’s tumor ….
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Two immunotherapeutic approaches showing promise in NSCLC are immune checkpoint inhibition and cancer vaccination.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Multiple agents are currently in advanced clinical development, and the results of ongoing randomized clinical trials will define the role of immunotherapy in the treatment of NSCLC”.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a phase II clinical trial, Morse et al (2013) examined if 1 of 2 vaccines based on dendritic cells (DCs) and poxvectors encoding CEA (carcinoembryonic antigen) and MUC1 (PANVAC) would lengthen survival in patients with resected metastases of colorectal cancer (CRC).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients, disease-free after CRC metastasectomy and peri-operative chemotherapy (n = 74), were randomized to injections of autologous DCs modified with PANVAC (DC/PANVAC) or PANVAC with per injection GM-CSF (granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: End-points were recurrence-free survival, overall survival (OS), and rate of CEA-specific immune responses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Recurrence-free survival at 2 years was similar (47 % and 55 % for DC/PANVAC and PANVAC/GM-CSF, respectively) (χ P = 0.48).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Ding and Ming (2014) noted that lung cancer is the most common malignancy worldwide in terms of incidence and mortality.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Immunotherapy consists of mainly therapeutic vaccination designed to induce or amplify the immune responses directed against tumor- associated antigens.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The vaccine group had produced significant improvement in OS compared with the control group (hazard ratio [HR] 0.83, 95 % confidence interval [CI]: 0.76 to 0.91; Z = 3.79, p = 0.0002].
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Subgroup analysis showed a more significant improvement of OS in the subgroup compared with the control group (HR 0.70, 95 % CI: 0.59 to 0.82; Z = 4.42, p < 0.00001).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No increased incidence of adverse events was obtained in the therapeutic vaccination group compared with the control group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that therapeutic vaccination added benefits to NSCLC patients and may become a standard complementary therapeutic approach in the future if the associated toxicity is reduced.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that immunotherapy may improve the survival of patients with GBM.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Kobayashi et al (2014) stated that DC-based cancer vaccines may have a significant benefit to patients with advanced pancreatic cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The mean survival time from diagnosis was 16.5 months (95 % CI: 14.4 to 18.5) and that from the first vaccination was 9.9 months (95 % CI: 8.0 to 12.9).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Known prognostic baseline factors related to advanced pancreatic cancer, namely ECOG-PS, peritoneal metastasis, liver metastasis, and the prognostic nutrition index, were also representative.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Helper Multi-Peptide (6MHP) Vaccine for Metastatic Melanoma Hu and colleagues (2015) stated that a multi-peptide vaccine (6MHP), designed to induce helper T cells against melanocytic and cancer-testis antigens, has been shown to induce specific Th1-dominant CD4+ T cell responses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers compared the long-term outcome of patients with metastatic melanoma vaccinated with 6MHP to that of a group of unvaccinated historical controls.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The 6MHP vaccine was given to patients with metastatic melanoma.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Circulating CD4+ T cell responses were measured by proliferation or direct IFN-gamma ELIspot assay.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Overall survival of vaccinated patients was compared to a group of clinically comparable historical controls using multi-variable Cox regression analysis and Kaplan-Meier survival analysis, taking into account age, metastatic site, and resection status.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Across 40 vaccinated patients and 87 controls, resection status (HR 0.54, p = 0.004) and vaccination (HR 0.24, p < 0.001) were associated with improved OS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Forty pairs of vaccinated patients and controls were matched by metastatic site, resection status, and age within 10 years.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Median survival was significantly longer for vaccinated patients (5.4 versus 1.3 years, p < 0.001).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Among the vaccinated patients, the development of a specific immune response after vaccination was associated with improved survival (HR 0.35, p = 0.040).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that helper peptide vaccination was associated with improved OS among patients with metastatic melanoma; they stated that these data support a randomized prospective trial of the 6MHP vaccine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Tumor-Associated Antigenic Peptide-Based Vaccines for Cancers Peres Lde and associates (2015) discussed peptide-based vaccines in breast cancer, immune responses and clinical outcomes, which include studies on animal models and phase I, phase I/II, phase II and phase III clinical trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers stated that peptide-based vaccines are powerful neoadjuvant immunotherapies that can directly target proteins expressed in tumor cells, mainly tumor-associated antigens (TAAs).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The most common breast cancer TAA epitopes are derived from MUC1, HER2/neu and CEA proteins.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Peptides derived from TAAs could be successfully used to elicit CD8 and CD4 T cell-specific responses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Thus, choosing peptides that adapt to natural variations of HLA genes is critical.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The most attractive advantage is that the target response is more specific and less toxic than for other therapies and vaccines.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Prominent studies on NeuVax - E75 (epitope for HER2/neu and GM-CSF) in breast cancer and DPX-0907 (HLA-A2-TAAs) expressed in breast cancer, ovarian and prostate cancer have shown the efficacy of peptide-based vaccines as neoadjuvant immunotherapy against cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Nishimura and co-workers (2015) noted that recent genome-wide cDNA microarray analysis of gene expression profiles in comprehensive tumor types coupled with isolation of cancer tissues by laser- microbeam microdissection have revealed ideal TAAs that are frequently over-expressed in various cancers including head and neck squamous cell cancer (HNSCC) and lung cancer, but not in most normal tissues except for testis, placenta, and fetal organs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Pre-clinical studies using HLA-transgenic mice and human T cells in-vitro showed that TAA-derived CTL-epitope short peptides (SPs) are highly immunogenic and induce HLA-A2 or -A24-restricted CTLs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Based on the accumulated evidence, these researchers carried out a phase II clinical trial of the TAA-SP vaccine in advanced 37 HNSCC patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This study showed a significant induction of TAA-specific CTLs in the majority of patients without serious adverse effects.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Another phase II clinical trial of therapeutic TAA-SP vaccine, designed to evaluate the ability of prevention of recurrence, is ongoing in HNSCC patients who have received curative operations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Further studies in human pre-clinical studies and in-vivo studies using HLA class I transgenic mice showed TAA-derived long peptides (TAA-LPs) have the capacity to induce not only promiscuous HLA class II-restricted CD4(+) T helper type 1 cells but also tumor-specific CTLs through a cross-presentation mechanism.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Moreover, these investigators observed an augmentation of TAA-LP-specific T helper type 1 cell responses and tumor antigen-spreading in HNSCC patients vaccinated with TAA-SPs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Chamani and colleagues (2018) noted that the E75 peptide vaccine, derived from tumor-associated antigen HER2, is the most frequently studied anti-HER2 vaccination strategy for the treatment of breast cancer patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Meta-analysis of delayed-type hypersensitivity (DTH) reactions and CD8+-T cell levels, as immune responses, displayed the significant differences in the vaccinated groups compared to their non-vaccinated counterparts.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: HLA-A24-Binding Peptide Vaccines for Gastric Cancer Fujiwara and colleagues (2017) performed a clinical trial using HLA-A24-binding peptide vaccines containing a combination of novel cancer-testis antigens and anti-angiogenic peptides for advanced gastric cancer (GC).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 35 GC patients who had shown resistance to the standard therapy were enrolled in this clinical trial using vaccinations with a mixture of multiple peptides derived from DEPDC1, URLC10, FoxM1, Kif20A and VEGFR1.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The safety, OS, and the immunological responses based on an ELISPOT assay were determined to assess differences in patients who were HLA-A24-positive [24(+)] and HLA-A24- negative [24(-)].
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No severe adverse events (AEs) were observed except for severe skin reactions in 4 patients.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that peptide vaccine therapy was found to be safe and is expected to induce specific T cell responses in patients with advanced GC.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They stated that the survival benefit of peptide vaccine monotherapy may not have been shown and further trials are needed to confirm these results.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: p16(INK4a)-Based Peptide Vaccine for Human Papillomavirus-Associated Cancers Reuschenbach and colleagues (2016) stated that the cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor p16(INK4a) is strongly and consistently over-expressed in all human papillomavirus (HPV)-associated cancers.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: To test this hypothesis, these investigators performed a phase I/IIa first-in-human trial to evaluate the safety and immunogenicity of a p16(INK4a)-based peptide vaccine.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 26 patients with different, advanced, p16(INK4a)-overexpressing, HPV DNA-positive cancers were included after the completion of standard treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: According to protocol, 12 subcutaneous injections of a p16(INK4) peptide (P16_37-63) mixed in a water-in-oil emulsion with immuno-adjuvant activity (Montanide ISA-51 VG) were administered over a 6- month period.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Clusters of differentiation (CD) 4 T cells were detected in 14 of 20 patients (3 of whom had pre-existing CD4 T cells before vaccination), CD8 T cells were detected in 5 of 20 patients, and antibodies were detected in 14 of 20 patients (1 of whom had pre-existing antibodies).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that vaccination with the p16(INK4a -derived peptide P16_37-63 appeared to induce cellular and humoral immune responses and did not cause severe toxicities.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They stated that the findings of the current study paved the way for the further clinical development of p16(INK4a -based cancer immuno-therapeutics.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: p62 DNA Vaccine (Elenagen) for Solid Tumors (e.g., Breast, Kidney, Lung, and Ovary Cancers as well as Melanoma) Ponomarenko and colleagues (2017) noted that Elenagen is a plasmid encoding p62/SQSTM1, the first DNA vaccine possessing 2 mutually complementing mechanisms of action: I. it elicits immune response against p62, and II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: it mitigates systemic chronic inflammation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: I. it elicits immune response against p62, and II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: it mitigates systemic chronic inflammation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Previously, Elenagen demonstrated anti-tumor safety and effectivness in rodent tumor models and spontaneous tumors in dogs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that Elenagen demonstrated good safety profile and ant-itumor activity in advanced solid tumors; especially encouraging is its ability to restore tumor sensitivity to chemotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vaccine for Ovarian Cancer In a pilot clinical trial, Tanyi and co-workers (2018) tested a personalized vaccine generated by autologous DCs pulsed with oxidized autologous whole-tumor cell lysate (OCDC), which was injected intranodally in platinum-treated, immunotherapy-naïve, recurrent ovarian cancer patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vaccination also amplified T cell responses against mutated neoepitopes derived from non-synonymous somatic tumor mutations, and this included priming of T cells against previously unrecognized neoepitopes, as well as novel T cell clones of markedly higher avidity against previously recognized neoepitopes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the use of oxidized whole-tumor lysate DC vaccine is safe and effective in eliciting a broad anti-tumor immunity, including private neoantigens, and warrants further clinical testing.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: WT1-specific cytotoxic T-lymphocytes were detected in 7 patients, and WT1 and HLA class I antigens were positive in all 34 cases.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study clarified the safety and potential acquisition of immunity after vaccination targeting WT1.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Chemotherapy, especially gemcitabine, is generally used for the treatment of advanced pancreatic cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Despite the treatment, the fatality rate for advanced pancreatic cancer is alarmingly high; therefore, there is a need for better therapeutic alternatives that has drawn focus to cancer vaccinations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Wilms tumor gene (WT1), typically associated with Wilms tumor, is found to be excessively expressed in some cancers, such as pancreatic cancer.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This characteristic feature is harvested to develop cancer vaccines against WT1.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They carried out a literature search on databases Medline, Web of Science, ScienceDirect, and Google Scholar using the keywords "advanced pancreatic cancer", "cancer vaccines", "WT1 vaccines," and "pulsed DC vaccines", and the results were studied to construct this review.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: WT1 vaccines work by introducing peptides from the WT1 protein to trigger an immune response involving cytotoxic T lymphocytes via antigen-presenting cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Upon activation, these lymphocytes induce apoptosis in cancer cells by targeting those with increased WT1 levels.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: WT1 vaccinations, which are usually administered in addition to chemotherapy, have showed clinically positive results and minimal side effects.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Targeting the over-expressed WT1 strategically highlighted a prospective option for tailored therapies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Vaccine for Gallbladder Cancer Rojas-Sepulveda and colleagues (2018) stated that immunotherapy based on check-point blockers has proven survival benefits in patients with melanoma and other malignancies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In these studies, 60 % of stage IV melanoma DC-treated patients showed immunological responses correlating with improved survival.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: By characterizing 9 different GBCCLs and several fresh tumor tissues, these researchers found that they expressed some tumor-associated antigens such as CEA, MUC-1, CA19-9, Erb2, Survivin, and several CEAs.
sentences.label: PREMISE
